ambiguities: References 'Claude Code CLI' with flags like --dangerously-skip-permissions and --bare but the specific environment requirements and installation method are unclear
ENV vars BEDROCK_URL and BEDROCK_API_KEY suggest custom AWS API Gateway but the actual infrastructure setup is not documented in the repo
Database initialization logic uses idempotent migrations with error suppression, but the migration ordering/versioning strategy is unclear for production deploys
The scan engine caches analysis using git HEAD hashes but the cache invalidation strategy for force-pushed branches is unclear
Task dependencies (dependsOn field) are stored as JSON but the dependency resolution and task ordering algorithm is not implemented in the visible code

architecturePatterns: Single-page application with Next.js App Router and client-side state management
Server-sent events (SSE) for real-time scan progress streaming
Background job processing with status polling (jobs table tracks async operations)
Multi-phase workflow state machine (analyze → plan → design → implement → done) with phase locking
Knowledge base generation as code - markdown wiki compiled from JSON analysis
Repo-level locking using in-memory mutex (Map<string, Promise<void>>) to prevent concurrent git operations
Dual AI integration strategy - prefers Claude Code CLI for file-reading capabilities, falls back to API with file stuffing
Encrypted credential storage using AES-256-GCM with per-credential IVs
Wave-based task decomposition with dependency tracking (stored as JSON arrays)
Git-based caching using HEAD commit hashes to avoid re-analysis of unchanged repos
Retry logic with exponential backoff for Azure DevOps and Bedrock API calls
Native HTTPS via curl/execFile to bypass Next.js fetch patching
businessFeatures: Automated codebase scanning and analysis using Claude to understand repository purpose, architecture, and dependencies
Cross-repository system synthesis to map service relationships and data flows
Knowledge base generation with bidirectional wiki links organized by architecture, features, flows, clusters, data model, and repos
Jira ticket synchronization supporting both Cloud and Server/Data Center deployments
AI-powered ticket analysis with conversational refinement (choice-based chat interface)
PRD (Product Requirements Document) generation from ticket analysis
Wave-based task decomposition with automatic repo assignment
Automated code implementation using Claude Code CLI to edit files directly in git branches
Pull request creation and management in Azure DevOps
Code review workflow with inline comments and priority levels
Scan findings/suggestions with dismiss and promote-to-ticket actions
Interview-based knowledge enrichment - user provides context that supplements automated analysis
PAT (Personal Access Token) encryption and secure storage
Repository selection and branch override configuration
Unified diff parsing and visualization for code changes

dataEntities.name: workspace
dataEntities.description: Top-level configuration container storing Azure DevOps org URL, Jira URL, project key, and done status mappings

dataEntities.name: pats
dataEntities.description: Encrypted Personal Access Tokens for Azure DevOps and Jira, with AES-256-GCM encryption and per-record IVs

dataEntities.name: repos
dataEntities.description: Repositories synced from Azure DevOps with selection status, branch overrides, and last scan timestamps

dataEntities.name: tickets
dataEntities.description: Jira issues synced locally with workflow phase tracking (analyze/plan/design/implement/done) and phase locking

dataEntities.name: waves
dataEntities.description: Ordered groups of related tasks within a ticket, implementing wave-based rollout strategy

dataEntities.name: tasks
dataEntities.description: Individual implementation units with repo assignments, branch names, PR URLs, diffs, and dependency chains

dataEntities.name: jobs
dataEntities.description: Background job tracker for long-running operations (scan, implement) with progress percentage and status

dataEntities.name: scanSuggestions
dataEntities.description: Critical and high-severity findings from codebase analysis with dismiss and promote-to-ticket capabilities

dataEntities.name: chatMessages
dataEntities.description: Conversational AI interactions during analyze and plan phases with optional choice buttons

dataEntities.name: interviewNotes
dataEntities.description: User-provided context transcripts with extracted structured facts for knowledge base enrichment

dataEntities.name: reviewComments
dataEntities.description: Code review feedback on tasks with file/line references, priority, and status tracking

findings.title: Credentials stored in .env.local file
findings.description: The .env.local file contains plaintext Azure DevOps PATs, Jira tokens, and API keys. While this file is gitignored, it represents a security risk if the file is backed up, logged, or accessed by unauthorized processes. Consider using a secrets manager or at minimum document rotation procedures.

findings.title: Dangerous Claude CLI flags in production code
findings.description: The claudeExec function uses --dangerously-skip-permissions flag which bypasses sandboxing. While documented in the code, this could allow arbitrary file system access during AI-driven code generation if the prompt injection attack surface is not carefully managed.

findings.title: Git operations lack error recovery for detached HEAD states
findings.description: The implement phase performs git checkout/branch operations but if the repo is in a detached HEAD state or has merge conflicts, the error handling may leave the repo in an inconsistent state. The cleanup logic in catch blocks attempts branch deletion but doesn't verify starting state.

findings.title: No database migrations or schema versioning
findings.description: The database uses idempotent DDL with try/catch on ALTER TABLE statements. This works for column additions but cannot handle column renames, type changes, or data migrations. Production deployments could fail or corrupt data if schema changes require more complex migrations.

findings.title: Unbounded git clone operations can exhaust disk space
findings.description: The scan engine clones repositories with --depth 1 but never prunes old clones. For large monorepos or many repositories, the kb/repos directory could grow to consume all available disk. No cleanup or LRU eviction strategy is implemented.

integrations.protocol: REST API v7.1 with Basic auth (PAT)
REST API v3 with Basic auth (email:token)
REST API v2 with Bearer auth (PAT)
HTTPS with API key (custom gateway wrapper)
Local process execution via execFile with JSON output

keyFiles.purpose: Drizzle ORM schema definitions for all database tables - central data model
Database initialization, DDL execution, and migration logic with singleton pattern
Core scanning orchestration - clones repos, runs Claude analysis, synthesizes cross-repo knowledge
Compiles raw scan output into organized wiki with bidirectional links across architecture/features/data model
Wrapper for Claude Code CLI and git operations with JSON schema support and repo locking
Claude API client supporting both Bedrock Converse and direct Anthropic API with retry logic
Azure DevOps REST API client for repos, PRs, file trees - uses native HTTPS to bypass Next.js proxy
Jira REST API client supporting both Cloud and Server with JQL search and pagination
AES-256-GCM encryption/decryption for PAT storage with key derivation from ENCRYPTION_KEY env var
Background implementation job - uses Claude CLI to edit code, creates git branches, generates diffs
Wave and task decomposition endpoint - breaks PRD into waves with repo assignments
Server-sent events endpoint for streaming scan progress with repo-level granularity
Main ticket UI orchestrator - renders phase-specific components (analyze/plan/design/implement) with progress tracking
Implementation phase UI - task list with status, diff viewer, PR creation, manual implementation toggle
Dependencies and scripts - Next.js 16, React 19, Drizzle ORM, better-sqlite3, Tailwind

purpose: DevCycle is an AI-powered software development workflow automation tool that integrates with Azure DevOps and Jira to streamline the entire development lifecycle. It automatically scans codebases to build a comprehensive knowledge base, then uses Claude AI to analyze tickets, generate implementation plans, write code, and create pull requests - all within a structured multi-phase workflow (analyze → plan → design → implement → done). The system combines codebase understanding with ticket management to accelerate development while maintaining code quality through automated analysis and review workflows.
techStack: Claude AI via AWS Bedrock Converse API
